summary: Validated /app/install.sh against the originally-reported SyntaxError bug in the api_keys.py codegen. All 6 backend tests pass: install.sh exists & passes `bash -n`; the two here-docs (unquoted config + quoted PYEOF sync engine) render a Python file that py_compiles even with a hostile EMAIL_JWT_SECRET containing BOTH a backslash and a double-quote; the escape line `sv.replace("\\", "\\\\").replace('\"', '\\"')` is preserved verbatim in the rendered file (this was the originally-mangled line); sync_all() writes ONLY backend/.env and frontend/.env (verified app.json + adConfig.ts sentinels are untouched); frontend/.env contains all required EXPO_PUBLIC_ADMOB_* keys plus EXPO_PUBLIC_ANDROID_PACKAGE; and round-tripping the hostile secret through the dotenv writer preserves it byte-for-byte. Live backend smoke (GET {EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL}/api/) returns 200.
critical_code_review_comments: install.sh correctly uses two here-docs: unquoted <<EOF for config (expands $(py_quote ...)) and quoted <<'PYEOF' for the static sync engine (preserves backslashes). This is the documented fix for the original SyntaxError.
py_quote() uses json.dumps to safely produce Python string literals for any input — robust against backslashes, double-quotes, unicode, etc.
sync_all() touches only the two git-ignored .env files; no tracked source (app.json, adConfig.ts) is rewritten — matches the design intent of api_keys.py being the single source of truth.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_install_codegen.py (new — pytest suite that extracts the install.sh here-docs and runs them in a temp dir with hostile inputs)
seed_data_creation: None. The pytest fixture renders api_keys.py into a per-test tmp_path; no production data is touched.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Re-run with: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_install_codegen.py -v. The suite is hermetic — it extracts the api_keys.py-generation here-docs from /app/install.sh, runs them under bash in a tmp dir with a hostile EMAIL_JWT_SECRET (backslash + double-quote), then imports the result and exercises sync_all(). If install.sh is ever refactored, the regex anchors `cat > "$API_KEYS_FILE" <<EOF` and `cat >> "$API_KEYS_FILE" <<'PYEOF'` must remain or the extraction needs updating.
rca_of_issue: Original bug: install.sh wrote api_keys.py via a single UNQUOTED here-doc (<<EOF). Bash inside an unquoted here-doc collapses `\\` to `\`, so the Python source line `sv.replace("\\", "\\\\")` became `sv.replace("\", "\\")` after generation — a literal `\"` inside the Python string, which Python parses as an unterminated escape, raising `SyntaxError: unexpected character after line continuation character` at the offending line (~82). Fix verified: the static sync engine is now written through a QUOTED here-doc (<<'PYEOF') where bash does ZERO expansion or escape processing, so backslashes survive verbatim. The config block remains unquoted, but py_quote() (json.dumps via python3) produces strings that don't rely on bash to preserve their escapes.
